tabulate.header:
import pytest
from tabulate import tabulate
from collections import namedtuple
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from dataclasses import dataclass

funcs.test_tabulate_empty_and_none_data_s0:
def test_tabulate_empty_and_none_data_s0():
    # None treated as empty list
    result_none = tabulate(None)
    result_empty = tabulate([])
    # Both should produce empty string or just a headerless empty table (spec doesn't specify exact output, 
    # but they should be equal and empty or minimal)
    assert isinstance(result_none, str)
    assert result_none == result_empty
    # Should be empty or whitespace only (no rows)
    assert result_none.strip() == ""
funcs.test_tabulate_headers_firstrow_and_keys_:
def test_tabulate_headers_firstrow_and_keys_with_list_of_lists_s0():
    data = [
        ["Name", "Age", "Score"],
        ["Alice", 30, 85.5],
        ["Bob", 25, 92.3],
    ]
    # headers="firstrow" uses first row as header and removes it from data
    result_firstrow = tabulate(data, headers="firstrow", tablefmt="plain")
    # Should contain header row "Name Age Score"
    assert "Name" in result_firstrow and "Age" in result_firstrow and "Score" in result_firstrow
    # Should NOT contain the first row as data
    assert "Alice" in result_firstrow and "Bob" in result_firstrow
    assert "Name" not in result_firstrow.splitlines()[1]  # header only in first line

    # headers="keys" with list-of-lists uses column indices as headers (0,1,2)
    result_keys = tabulate(data[1:], headers="keys", tablefmt="plain")
    # Should contain headers "0", "1", "2"
    assert "0" in result_keys and "1" in result_keys and "2" in result_keys
    # Should contain data rows
    assert "Alice" in result_keys and "Bob" in result_keys
funcs.test_tabulate_tablefmt_variants_produce_:
def test_tabulate_tablefmt_variants_produce_different_outputs_s0():
    data = [["A", 1], ["B", 2]]
    plain = tabulate(data, tablefmt="plain")
    grid = tabulate(data, tablefmt="grid")
    pipe = tabulate(data, tablefmt="pipe")
    # Outputs should be different strings
    assert plain != grid and grid != pipe and plain != pipe
    # grid output should contain + and | characters
    assert "+" in grid and "|" in grid
    # pipe output should contain | but no +
    assert "|" in pipe and "+" not in pipe
    # plain output should not contain + or |
    assert "+" not in plain and "|" not in plain
funcs.test_tabulate_float_and_int_formatting_s:
def test_tabulate_float_and_int_formatting_s0():
    data = [
        ["Item", "Price", "Qty"],
        ["Apple", 1.2345, 10],
        ["Banana", 0.5, 5],
    ]
    # Use headers="firstrow" to get headers
    # floatfmt and intfmt as format specifiers
    result = tabulate(data, headers="firstrow", floatfmt=".2f", intfmt="04d", tablefmt="plain")
    # Prices should be formatted to 2 decimals
    assert "1.23" in result and "0.50" in result
    # Quantities should be zero-padded to 4 digits
    assert "0010" in result and "0005" in result
funcs.test_tabulate_showindex_variants_s0:
def test_tabulate_showindex_variants_s0():
    data = [["a", 1], ["b", 2]]
    # showindex="always" adds 0-based index column
    result_always = tabulate(data, showindex="always", tablefmt="plain")
    assert result_always.splitlines()[0].startswith("0") or result_always.splitlines()[1].startswith("0")
    # showindex="never" disables index column
    result_never = tabulate(data, showindex="never", tablefmt="plain")
    # Should not contain "0" or "1" as index column at start of lines
    lines = result_never.splitlines()
    assert not any(line.startswith("0") or line.startswith("1") for line in lines if line.strip())
    # showindex as custom iterable
    result_custom = tabulate(data, showindex=["x", "y"], tablefmt="plain")
    # Should contain "x" and "y" as first column values
    assert "x" in result_custom and "y" in result_custom
funcs.test_tabulate_empty_and_none_data_s1:
def test_tabulate_empty_and_none_data_s1():
    # None treated as empty list
    result_none = tabulate(None)
    result_empty = tabulate([])
    assert result_none == result_empty
    # Both should produce just an empty table (no rows, no headers)
    assert result_none.strip() == ""
funcs.test_tabulate_headers_firstrow_excludes_:
def test_tabulate_headers_firstrow_excludes_first_row_from_data_s1():
    data = [
        ["Name", "Age", "City"],
        ["Alice", 30, "NY"],
        ["Bob", 25, "LA"],
    ]
    result = tabulate(data, headers="firstrow")
    # The header row should be the first row of data, excluded from output rows
    assert "Name" in result
    assert "Age" in result
    assert "City" in result
    assert "Alice" in result
    assert "Bob" in result
    assert "Name" not in result.splitlines()[2]  # header only in first line, not repeated in data
funcs.test_tabulate_headers_keys_with_dict_of_:
def test_tabulate_headers_keys_with_dict_of_iterables_s1():
    data = {
        "col1": [1, 2],
        "col2": ["a", "b"]
    }
    result = tabulate(data, headers="keys")
    # Headers should be the dict keys
    assert "col1" in result
    assert "col2" in result
    # Data rows should contain values
    assert "1" in result
    assert "a" in result
funcs.test_tabulate_showindex_options_s1:
def test_tabulate_showindex_options_s1():
    data = [["a", 1], ["b", 2]]
    # Default: no explicit index column if data has no index
    result_default = tabulate(data)
    assert "0" not in result_default.splitlines()[0]  # header line no index column
    # showindex=True adds index column
    result_always = tabulate(data, showindex=True)
    assert "0" in result_always
    assert "1" in result_always
    # showindex=False disables index column
    result_never = tabulate(data, showindex=False)
    assert "0" not in result_never
funcs.test_tabulate_tablefmt_grid_and_plain_s1:
def test_tabulate_tablefmt_grid_and_plain_s1():
    data = [["a", "b"], ["c", "d"]]
    # grid format includes visible grid characters
    result_grid = tabulate(data, tablefmt="grid")
    assert "+" in result_grid and "|" in result_grid
    # plain format is minimal, no grid chars
    result_plain = tabulate(data, tablefmt="plain")
    assert "+" not in result_plain
    assert "|" not in result_plain
    # simple format default includes minimal borders
    result_simple = tabulate(data, tablefmt="simple")
    assert "-" in result_simple or "|" in result_simple
funcs.test_empty_table_and_none_tabular_data_s:
def test_empty_table_and_none_tabular_data_s3():
    # None treated as empty table
    assert tabulate(None) == ""
    # Empty list treated as empty table
    assert tabulate([]) == ""
funcs.test_showindex_behavior_s3:
def test_showindex_behavior_s3():
    data = [["a", 1], ["b", 2]]
    # showindex="always" adds 0-based integer index column
    out_always = tabulate(data, showindex="always")
    assert "0" in out_always and "1" in out_always
    # showindex=False disables index column
    out_never = tabulate(data, showindex=False)
    assert "0" not in out_never or "1" not in out_never
    # showindex=custom iterable uses that as index column
    out_custom = tabulate(data, showindex=["x", "y"])
    assert "x" in out_custom and "y" in out_custom
funcs.test_preserve_whitespace_option_s3:
def test_preserve_whitespace_option_s3():
    data = [[" a ", "b\tc"], [" d ", "e f"]]
    # By default, whitespace is stripped or normalized
    out_default = tabulate(data)
    # With preserve_whitespace=True, whitespace preserved
    out_preserve = tabulate(data, preserve_whitespace=True)
    assert out_preserve != out_default
    # Check that whitespace characters appear in output when preserved
    assert " a " in out_preserve or "b\tc" in out_preserve or " d " in out_preserve or "e f" in out_preserve
